title: lepidocupania concolor
intro: Lepidocupania concolor, also known as the White Cup Plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Africa and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as to aid in digestion and relieve menstrual cramps. In herbal preparations, Lepidocupania concolor can be made into a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to create a syrup or tincture for medicinal purposes.
